aed.title: Defibrillator (AED)
steps: AED arrived — turn it ON and follow its voice
Bare the chest; dry it if wet, shave very hairy skin if a razor is in the kit
Stick pads on bare skin: one upper-right chest, one lower-left side
Plug the pads in; let the AED analyse — DON'T TOUCH the patient
Shock advised? Shout 'clear', make sure no one touches, press the flashing button
Resume CPR immediately — push to the beat. It re-checks every 2 minutes

cpr.title: CPR — cardiac arrest
steps: Tap the shoulders, shout — any response?
No normal breathing? Start CPR now
Heel of hand on the centre of the chest, other hand on top
Push HARD & FAST — 5–6 cm deep, 110 per minute
Don't stop. Swap rescuer every 2 minutes if you tire

breaths.title: Rescue breaths (30:2)
steps: After 30 compressions, give 2 rescue breaths
Tilt the head back, lift the chin, pinch the nose
Seal your mouth over theirs — 2 slow breaths (1s each)
Watch the chest rise, then resume compressions

breathing.title: Check breathing / airway
steps: Tilt the head back, lift the chin
Look, listen and feel for breathing — 10 seconds
Breathing normally? Put them in the recovery position
Not breathing normally? Start CPR

bleeding.title: Severe bleeding
steps: Press HARD directly on the wound with cloth or your hands
Pack a deep wound with gauze/cloth and keep pressing
Limb bleed that won't stop? Apply a tourniquet high & tight
Write down the time the tourniquet went on

recovery.title: Recovery position
steps: Kneel beside them
Place the far arm across the chest, the near arm out at a right angle
Roll them toward you onto their side
Tilt the head back to keep the airway open; check breathing
